concepts.label: Cryptocurrency
concepts.reason: The header introduces the main domain-specific topic of the document.

paragraphs.text: Bitcoin was created in 2008 by an anonymous person or group using the pseudonym Satoshi Nakamoto. The whitepaper titled "Bitcoin: A Peer-to-Peer Electronic Cash System" introduced a decentralized digital currency that operates without central banks or intermediaries.

concepts.label: Decentralized digital currency without intermediaries

concepts.reason: This phrase explains what Bitcoin is and how it operates, making it a definitional concept worth grounding.

concepts.label: Blockchain technology
concepts.reason: The header introduces the central domain-specific topic of the section.

paragraphs.text: A blockchain is a distributed ledger that records all transactions across a network of computers. Each block contains a cryptographic hash of the previous block, creating an immutable chain. Bitcoin's blockchain processes roughly 7 transactions per second, while Visa handles around 24,000.

concepts.label: Bitcoin vs Visa transaction throughput
concepts.searchQuery: Bitcoin blockchain transactions per second Visa transactions per second 7 24000
concepts.reason: This is a quantitative comparison between two payment systems that should be verified against authoritative sources.
surfaceForms: Bitcoin's blockchain processes roughly 7 transactions per second

concepts.label: Hash-linked block structure
concepts.reason: This describes the core mechanism by which blocks are linked and why the chain is considered immutable.
surfaceForms: cryptographic hash of the previous block

concepts.label: Blockchain
concepts.reason: The paragraph explicitly defines what a blockchain is in concise technical terms.

paragraphs.text: The consensus mechanism Bitcoin uses is called Proof of Work, where miners compete to solve complex mathematical puzzles. The first miner to solve the puzzle adds the next block and receives newly minted bitcoin as a reward. This reward started at 50 BTC and halves approximately every four years.

concepts.label: Bitcoin block reward halving schedule
concepts.searchQuery: Bitcoin block reward started at 50 BTC and halves approximately every four years
concepts.reason: Contains specific factual details about the initial reward amount and halving interval that are verifiable.

concepts.label: Block mining reward process
concepts.searchQuery: Bitcoin first miner to solve the puzzle adds the next block and receives newly minted bitcoin as a reward
concepts.reason: Describes the procedure by which a miner wins the right to add a block and earn a reward.

receives newly minted bitcoin as a reward

concepts.reason: Named consensus mechanism and core technical concept in the paragraph.

concepts.label: Ethereum and smart contracts
concepts.reason: Section header naming the main topic discussed in this paragraph.

paragraphs.text: Ethereum was proposed by Vitalik Buterin in 2013 and launched in 2015. It introduced smart contracts—self-executing programs stored on the blockchain that automatically enforce agreements when conditions are met.

concepts.reason: This is a specific historical claim with dates and attribution that can be verified.

paragraphs.text: In September 2022, Ethereum transitioned from Proof of Work to Proof of Stake in an event called "The Merge." This reduced Ethereum's energy consumption by approximately 99.95% according to the Ethereum Foundation.

concepts.searchQuery: Ethereum September 2022 transitioned from Proof of Work to Proof of Stake The Merge
concepts.reason: This is a dated, verifiable statement about a major protocol change and its named event.
surfaceForms: transitioned from Proof of Work to Proof of Stake

concepts.label: Post-Merge Ethereum energy consumption reduction

concepts.reason: This is a quantitative reported outcome with an attributed source that should be verified.

concepts.reason: A domain-specific topic header naming the subject area of the section.

paragraphs.text: DeFi applications allow users to lend, borrow, and trade without traditional financial intermediaries. The total value locked in DeFi protocols peaked at over $180 billion in late 2021 before declining significantly.

paragraphs.text: Uniswap, launched in 2018, pioneered the automated market maker model where liquidity providers deposit token pairs into pools. The protocol has processed over $1.8 trillion in trading volume since its launch.

paragraphs.text: The SEC has classified most cryptocurrencies as securities, with notable exceptions for Bitcoin and Ethereum. The landmark SEC v. Ripple case in 2023 established that programmatic sales of tokens on exchanges do not necessarily constitute securities transactions.

concepts.label: SEC classification of cryptocurrencies as securities with Bitcoin and Ethereum exceptions
concepts.searchQuery: SEC classified most cryptocurrencies as securities exceptions Bitcoin Ethereum
concepts.reason: This is a specific regulatory claim about how the SEC treats cryptocurrencies, including named exceptions that require verification.
